_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — take the L5 'Mid Scale Mail' keeper and apply the L5→L6 delta to produce the Lvl 6 'Heavy Scale Mail + Over-Jerkin + First Gold + Longbow Shape' variant (Tier 2 top, final pre-plate level). Bow shape evolution beat is the silhouette change at this level.
_meta.input_image_content: UnitProgression/Archer/out/v3/L5/composite/archer_L5_v<keeper>_composited.png (v3 chain, user picks keeper at runtime)
_meta.level_beat_doc: UnitProgression/Archer/Archer_Progression_Plan.md (L6 — Heavy Scale Mail + Over-Jerkin + First Gold + Longbow Shape; revised 2026-05-07 per AD FeedbackV0)
_meta.tier: Tier 2 — Scale Mail (top) — final pre-plate level
_meta.input_strategy: Within-tier chain (single-input chained, locked 2026-05-07) — previous keeper composite is the sole input. No L1_Base scale anchor.
history: v1-v3 (2026-05-04): see prior history — L5→L6 delta with over-jerkin + 3rd pouch/tool-loop + gold bracer trim + gold bow inlay + leather throat collar.
v_no_stubble (2026-05-05): Stripped 'faint stubble shadow' from face descriptions. Canonical face is now clean-shaven smooth peachy-tan skin with no facial hair.

v4_detail_economy (2026-05-07): MAJOR REWRITE per AD FeedbackV0 (CLAUDE.md Part 3 §23 + Part 2 §G). (a) BOW SHAPE EVOLUTION BEAT (NEW): bow limbs LENGTHEN ~20% toward longbow proportions — silhouette change is the L6 bow beat per AD 'short bow should evolve into a longbow'. (b) DROPPED third belt pouch + tool-loop ring + leather strop + sharpening-stone tag (AD belt-kit minimization — dagger sheath remains the only belt accessory). (c) DROPPED thin gold inlay line along inner curve of bow (replaced by the silhouette change; per Detail Economy rule, surface ornament cut). (d) KEPT over-jerkin (volume/material change at chest, preserves livery), leather throat collar (small but reads at neck), thin gold trim at bracer wrist edges (FIRST GOLD marker on the character). (e) PRESERVE block updated to reflect L5 v3 state: hood DOWN as red mantle, steel skullcap fully visible on top of head, scale-mail coif at back/sides of head (NOT at neck), plain leather pauldron caps (no studs), short scale-mail BAND below tunic (3-4 inches, NOT mid-thigh skirt), dagger sheath standalone (no pouches). (f) Updated paths to v3 chain. Adds 5 → 4.

v3_single_input (2026-05-07): Dropped L1_Base from image_urls per locked single-input chained rule (CLAUDE.md Part 1 §B revised). Stripped 'SECOND input image' / scale-anchor language from prompt body and FRAMING block heading. Sole input is the previous composited keeper. urls_before=2, urls_after=1, body_chars_removed=41.

v5_boot_progression (2026-05-08): Per AD feedback — boots get a progression starting at L6. Initial draft used brass cuff trim band + brass eyelets along the boot front lacing — REJECTED by AD (eyelets/lacing are surface trim, exactly the detail-economy trap). Reworked to plate-only (locked 2026-05-08): added new beat (5) SMALL STEEL TOE CAP — first plate on the boot, ~1 inch deep at the toe, polished cool silver-gray steel. Adds count 4 → 5.

adds.title: ADD A SHORT SLEEVELESS BROWN LEATHER OVER-JERKIN OVER THE TUNIC
adds.summary: Add a fitted brown leather sleeveless vest worn OVER THE TUNIC (not over the scale mail), covering ONLY THE UPPER-MID CHEST AREA.

adds.title: ADD A LEATHER THROAT COLLAR
adds.summary: add a small brown leather collar piece around the THROAT, framing the front of the neck BELOW the chin and ABOVE the upper edge of the over-jerkin.

adds.title: ADD A THIN GOLD TRIM LINE AT EACH BRACER WRIST EDGE (FIRST GOLD ON THE CHARACTER)
adds.summary: add a thin BUTTERY POLISHED YELLOW GOLD line running around the TOP RIM of EACH forearm bracer (both the bow-arm bracer and the draw-arm bracer), at the edge where the leather meets the bare-skin upper forearm.

adds.title: BOW UPGRADE
adds.summary: LIMBS LENGTHEN ~20% TOWARD LONGBOW PROPORTIONS (BOW SILHOUETTE EVOLUTION) — the bow in the input image is a SHORT RECURVE.

adds.title: SMALL STEEL TOE CAP ON EACH BOOT
adds.summary: First plate on the boots — a small polished cool silver-gray STEEL TOE CAP (~1 inch deep) wraps the front of each boot's toe. Plate accumulation, NOT eyelets or lacing or surface trim. Boots otherwise stay chunky warm-brown leather with the darker folded ankle cuff.

image_urls: <<UPLOAD: UnitProgression/Archer/out/v3/L5/composite/archer_L5_v<keeper>_composited.png>> (CONTENT REFERENCE — the v3 L5 keeper to upgrade)
prompt: EDIT GOAL — Apply FIVE additive upgrades to the archer in the input image (the 'Mid Scale Mail' content reference) to produce the 'Heavy Scale Mail + Over-Jerkin + First Gold + Longbow Shape + Boot Toe Cap' variant — the final scale-mail-tier step before the plate transition. The signature L6 silhouette change is the BOW LIMBS LENGTHENING toward longbow proportions; the boots also pick up their first plate accent (a small steel toe cap). KEEP EVERYTHING FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY AS-IS unless explicitly changed by the four upgrades below. CRITICAL — LIVERY PRESERVATION: the mustard-yellow tunic with the quilted red CENTER panel and quilted red SIDE panels is the EMTD livery and MUST REMAIN VISIBLY DOMINANT on the chest. The new over-jerkin is INTENTIONALLY SHORT and PARTIAL so the tunic stays prominently visible both above and below it. ===== NEW ADDS FOR THIS EDIT (first gold appears + bow shape evolves) ===== (1) ADD A SHORT SLEEVELESS BROWN LEATHER OVER-JERKIN OVER THE TUNIC — Add a fitted brown leather sleeveless vest worn OVER THE TUNIC (not over the scale mail), covering ONLY THE UPPER-MID CHEST AREA. The over-jerkin starts roughly at the upper chest just below the collarbones (NOT at the collar) and stops at MID-TORSO (at roughly the bottom of the breastbone), leaving CLEAR EXPOSED LIVERY both ABOVE and BELOW. ABOVE the over-jerkin, a clear band of the mustard tunic and the upper portions of the red center panel and red side panels are fully visible at the upper-chest / shoulder / collar area (the leather throat collar from add (2) below sits above this band at the neck). BELOW the over-jerkin, a clear band of the mustard tunic and the lower portions of the red panels are fully visible between the over-jerkin's bottom edge and the wider leather belt at the waist. AT LEAST 40-50% of the tunic's red+yellow surface area must remain visible after the over-jerkin is added. The over-jerkin is matte warm-brown leather, slightly darker than the bracers and pauldrons, with a clean leather chest-piece silhouette and a SINGLE small brass buckle on a short brown leather strap centered at the front. Sleeveless — stops at the shoulder line where the leather pauldron caps begin, leaving the scale-mail sleeves on the arms fully visible. The X-strap sits OVER the over-jerkin (the strap remains visible across the leather chest piece). (2) ADD A LEATHER THROAT COLLAR — add a small brown leather collar piece around the THROAT, framing the front of the neck BELOW the chin and ABOVE the upper edge of the over-jerkin. The collar is matte warm-brown leather, slightly darker than the over-jerkin, with a single visible seam and a clean rolled top edge. It sits at the front of the neck, integrating with the existing scale-mail coif (which frames the BACK and SIDES of the head) — together they form a complete neck-area material transition (steel skullcap → scale-mail coif at back/sides → leather collar at front of throat → over-jerkin at upper chest). (3) ADD A THIN GOLD TRIM LINE AT EACH BRACER WRIST EDGE (FIRST GOLD ON THE CHARACTER) — add a thin BUTTERY POLISHED YELLOW GOLD line running around the TOP RIM of EACH forearm bracer (both the bow-arm bracer and the draw-arm bracer), at the edge where the leather meets the bare-skin upper forearm. The line is thin and clean, painted as a single gilt accent stripe with subtle highlight. Gold rendering: BUTTERY POLISHED YELLOW GOLD METAL, saturated warm-yellow hue contrasting strongly against the surrounding warm-brown leather, distinctly more yellow than the leather and the bow's brass — NOT brass, NOT bronze, NOT yellow paint, NOT mustard tint. Render with: (i) saturated buttery-yellow base, (ii) soft top-down highlight reading almost white-yellow on the upper edge, (iii) deeper warm shadow on the underside reading honey-orange. Each gold line must be IMMEDIATELY READABLE as gold at thumbnail scale. This is the FIRST GOLD on the character — the unit's first-gold marker. (4) BOW UPGRADE — LIMBS LENGTHEN ~20% TOWARD LONGBOW PROPORTIONS (BOW SILHOUETTE EVOLUTION) — the bow in the input image is a SHORT RECURVE. In this edit each limb (BOTH the upper limb and the lower limb) is LENGTHENED by approximately 20% relative to the original short-recurve length, evolving the bow toward longbow proportions. The lengthening is symmetric — both limbs grow equally — and the new total bow length spans from roughly hip level (where the bow-arm hand grips the central leather grip wrap, unchanged) down past the upper thigh on the lower side and up past the wider leather belt on the upper side. The recurve curvature flattens slightly with the new length, reading as a transitional shape between a short hunting bow and a tall longbow. The grip itself stays at the SAME hip-level position in the bow-arm hand (the hand and grip do not move); only the limbs grow longer. The brass grip-end binding rings, the brass tip caps at both limb ends, and the warm wood-brown limb material all carry over to the lengthened bow — the new longer wood between the brass details remains warm wood-brown. (5) SMALL STEEL TOE CAP ON EACH BOOT — the chunky warm-brown leather boots from the input image gain their FIRST PLATE accent. Add a SMALL POLISHED COOL SILVER-GRAY STEEL TOE CAP wrapping the front toe of EACH boot — about 1 inch deep at the toe, painted as a smooth curved steel plate that conforms to the boot's toe shape with a clean curved upper edge where the steel meets the warm-brown leather body. The toe cap is a discrete piece of armor (plate accumulation), NOT a surface ornament. NO eyelets, NO front-lacing dots, NO visible shoelaces, NO brass cuff trim band, NO stitching rows, NO rivets along the cap edge, NO trim line wrapping the cuff — the boot evolution at this level is the addition of a small armor PLATE at the toe only. The boot otherwise remains chunky warm-brown leather with the darker folded ankle cuff exactly as in the input image. The toe cap is the first piece of plate armor on the boot; it carries through every subsequent level. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== Every other element of the input image must remain identical: face (chiseled chibi jawline + clean-shaven smooth peachy-tan skin + closed-mouth half-smirk + asymmetric brow with anatomical-LEFT brow higher + warm brown eyes with single catchlight + small upturned nose); the deep crimson-red CLOTH HOOD IS DOWN at the shoulders as a RED CLOTH MANTLE / SHORT COWL (carried unchanged from the input image — the hood is NOT pulled up, it is dropped at the shoulders and back of the neck as a red mantle layer); the steel skullcap fully visible on top of the head (polished cool silver-gray bowl shape covering the crown, two small painted iron rivets at the temples — carried unchanged); the scale-mail coif framing the BACK and SIDES of the head BELOW the skullcap (chunky overlapping silver-gray scales — carried unchanged; the coif covers the temples + behind the ears + nape of neck only, NOT the face / jaw / front of throat); matching crimson-red cape attached at both shoulders falling to knee length with soft folds and inward curl on viewer-LEFT; both forearms bracered (bow arm single cross-strap, draw arm two cross-strap bindings, now with the new thin gold trim at the top wrist edge); both hands BARE peachy-tan; bare anatomical-LEFT hand gripping the bow grip vertically at hip height; the bow with warm wood-brown limbs (now lengthened ~20% per add 4) + dark-brown leather grip wrap + brass grip-end binding rings + brass tip caps + pale cream bowstring; brown leather cylindrical back-quiver over anatomical-LEFT shoulder rising behind viewer-RIGHT side of head with three to four white-feather red-tip arrows; the mustard-yellow tunic with quilted red center panel and quilted red side panels visibly DOMINANT on the chest from collar to upper hip (carried unchanged — only the upper-mid portion is partially covered by the new short over-jerkin); SHORT-SLEEVE scale mail on both shoulders covering ONLY deltoid + upper bicep, ending mid-bicep in clean scalloped edge (unchanged); BARE-SKIN BAND of muscular bicep + elbow + upper forearm visible between the short scale-mail sleeve and the leather wrist bracer (unchanged); SHORT BAND of scale mail (3-4 inches tall) visible below the lower edge of the tunic hem (unchanged — NOT a long mid-thigh skirt); plain leather pauldron caps over both deltoids (slightly darker brown than the bracers, single seam over each cap, NO studs — pauldrons remain plain leather domes); the leather X-strap chest harness diagonally across the front (now visible on top of the new over-jerkin); wider single warm-brown leather belt with brass rectangular buckle and visible edge stitching; dark-wood dagger in leather sheath at the anatomical-RIGHT hip with brass capstone on handle (STANDALONE — no hip pouches, no satchels, no tool-loop rings accompany it); brown leather greaves on lower legs from boot cuff to just below knee with single cross-strap binding each; olive forest-green knee-length trousers (visible above the greaves); chunky warm-brown leather boots with the darker folded ankle cuff (now with the new SMALL STEEL TOE CAP wrapping the front toe per add 5; otherwise unchanged in silhouette and color). ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled 15-25 degrees to viewer's left, weight planted evenly on both feet with slight forward lean. THE BOW ARM (anatomical-LEFT, viewer-RIGHT) hangs DOWN at his side, bow-arm hand at HIP height gripping the central leather grip wrap (the grip stays at the same hip-level position even though the limbs are now longer). THE DRAW ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, viewer-LEFT) bent ~90 degrees, FOREARM HORIZONTAL ACROSS THE CHEST at upper-chest level, bracered hand at chest CENTER in TIGHT CLOSED FIST with KNUCKLES OUTWARD. Calm balanced HERO STANCE; NOT an active draw. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match these canonical numbers exactly) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Character at ~75% vertical fill, ~57% horizontal width. TOP of head (steel skullcap dome) at ~13.5% from top. BOTTOM of boots at ~12% from bottom. Roughly vertically centered. The lengthened bow limbs may extend slightly beyond the body silhouette but should not push the canvas top or bottom margins — the bow grip stays at hip level so the limbs grow into the existing torso/upper-leg space, not into the headroom or below the boots. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT add wider borders, DO NOT crop, DO NOT shift to bottom edge. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration, soft painted shading, warm subsurface skin, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones, soft painted highlights on metal and leather. Clean thin black silhouette outline in deep warm brown-black, even weight, slight thickening in shadow recesses, slight thinning under key light. Interior linework soft and value-driven. Clean white background, no environmental context, no ground plane, minimal contact shadow. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT pull the cloth hood back up — the hood at this level remains DOWN at the shoulders as a red mantle (carried over from the previous level). DO NOT replace the steel skullcap with a tall conical helm, an open-face plate helm, or a closed-visor helm — the skullcap stays as a simple polished steel bowl on top of the head. DO NOT cover the face / jaw / throat with the scale-mail coif — only the back and sides of the head + nape of neck. DO NOT add brass studs to the leather pauldrons — pauldrons stay PLAIN leather. DO NOT use FINE CHAINMAIL RING MESH or ring mesh or fish-scale fine texture — the visible scale mail (sleeves, body band, coif) must remain CHUNKY OVERLAPPING ROUNDED SCALES. DO NOT make the leather over-jerkin sleeved or extend it to mid-thigh — it is SLEEVELESS, SHORT, and stops at MID-TORSO. DO NOT make the over-jerkin metal or studded — it is plain matte warm-brown leather with a single buckled clasp. DO NOT cover more than 50% of the tunic — the mustard-yellow base + quilted red center panel + quilted red side panels MUST remain visibly DOMINANT on the chest both ABOVE and BELOW the over-jerkin (LIVERY PRESERVATION rule). DO NOT extend the over-jerkin up to the collar — it starts at the upper chest just below the collarbones, leaving an exposed band of tunic visible at the upper chest / shoulders / collar. DO NOT remove the mustard tunic / red panels. DO NOT add a crossbody arrow bandolier or any second crossbody strap — only the existing X-strap remains across the front of the chest. DO NOT add ANY hip pouch, belt pouch, leather satchel, tool-loop ring, leather strop, sharpening-stone tag, or hip-mounted accessory — the dagger sheath is the ONLY belt accessory at this level (belt-kit minimization rule). DO NOT add a third pouch in front of the belt buckle. DO NOT add steel pauldrons, steel breastplate, steel vambraces, or any plate armor. DO NOT add tassets or arming-skirt strips at the hip. DO NOT add a closed visor. DO NOT add a gold helm trim band. DO NOT add a gold belt buckle — the belt buckle stays brass. DO NOT add gold finial or gold spike on top of the head. DO NOT add gold tip caps to the bow limb tips — those tips remain BRASS. DO NOT add a thin gold inlay line along the inner curve of the bow limbs — at this level the bow's gold accent is NONE; the bow's L6 beat is the SILHOUETTE CHANGE (limbs lengthening), not a surface ornament. DO NOT make the entire bow gold. DO NOT add gold anywhere on the cape, hood, mantle, tunic, belt, boots, or trousers — at this level the only gold on the character is the thin trim line at each bracer wrist edge. DO NOT shorten the bow back to its original short-recurve length — at this level the limbs are ~20% LONGER, transitioning toward longbow proportions. DO NOT make the bow into a true full longbow that extends past the head or below the boots — it is a TRANSITIONAL ~20%-lengthened shape, still anchored at hip level via the unchanged grip. DO NOT add gemstones, jewels, embroidery, runes, or engraved patterns. DO NOT swap the hand holding the bow. DO NOT change the chibi proportions. DO NOT change any facial feature, hair, or skin tone. DO NOT change the cape color, length, or drape. DO NOT change the arrow fletching colors (white feather + red tips). DO NOT add or remove arrows. DO NOT change the back-quiver position. DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition. DO NOT crop the figure. DO NOT change the background or add scenery. DO NOT drift the art style. DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
